{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":319111,"duration_api_ms":319293,"ttft_ms":2645,"num_turns":47,"result":"Review complete. **Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** — CP00-403 is approved for closeout with no P0/P1/P2 issues and one non-blocking P3 metadata note.

Key results:
- **Consistency**: pack binding, requirements, no-write attestation, service factories, validators, contract projection, manifest, and the live plan all agree (Risk C, 150 units, RP13.P05.M04.S06–RP13.P06.M01.S12, upstream CP00-402, next CP00-404).
- **Boundaries**: descriptor-only invariants enforced per row; comprehensive no-write/no-real-data attestation; Hermes (H13) used only for evidence-packet/receipt descriptors, not product runtime.
- **Authority**: Claude stays read-only/non-final-approver; no enterprise-trust claim from local validation; production_ready correctly deferred (`production_ready: false`, `implemented_pending_review`).
- **Hardening**: auth preflight passed with valid JSON, forbidden tools absent, summary-only correctly prohibited, no pre-baked review output.

The single P3 flags that `changed-file-scope.json` double-classifies the pack's own manifest dir as both implementation and "unrelated" — benign review-tooling metadata, no deliverable impact. Verified strong internal consistency: the CP403 pack binding (registry.js:188), requirements (registry.js:1200), no-write attestation (registry.js:1194), service descriptor/case-set factories (service.js:1474,1507), validators (validators.js:2042-2201), contract projection (payments-core-contract.json current_pack, cp403_requirements, cp403_no_write_attestation, p05_closeout_p06_foundation_descriptor, historical_pack_bindings tail), manifest, and the live closeout-pack-plan all agree on pack_id, risk C, 150 units, range, upstream CP00-402, next CP00-404, next_subphase RP13.P06.M01.S13. Deliverable distribution (fixture 43, claude_review 7, implementation 57, security_audit 16, test 18, hermes_evidence 6, ui 3 = 150) and micro-phase/micro-title row counts match across all artifacts; independently spot-counted claude_review=7, ui=3, hermes_evidence=6, and confirmed M04 section rows map exactly to plan units S06-S20 with no off-by-one. All 7 required public exports are defined and re-exported through src/index.js and imported by the passing test suite. Descriptor-only invariants (descriptor_only true, runtime_execution false, customer_safe_errors_only true, raw_payload_included false) are enforced per-row by validatePaymentsCoreCp403P05CloseoutP06FoundationDescriptor. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensively attested (all dispatch_*_runtime, db/storage/audit/permission-write flags false; no_real_data true; emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false), so Hermes (H13) is used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors, not product runtime. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening true. The pack makes no affirmative production_ready claim (manifest production_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review; hermes production_ready_candidate:true reflects descriptor-coverage validity only), so there is no over-claim of enterprise trust. CP178+ review hardening checks pass: readiness auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1450-byte valid-JSON stdout), validation-report shows forbidden_tools_present false and summary_only_review_allowed false (correctly prohibited for Risk C + security/review deliverables), prompt within size limit, source_inspection_mode read_tools_used, and no pre-baked raw-output.json/review-receipt.json exist (this review is fresh). command-evidence records npm test, npm run validate, rp13:payments-core:validate, closeout-pack-plan:validate, weighted/spec/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane validate, build, and git diff --check all at exit 0, with post-review gated commands correctly deferred. One non-blocking P3 metadata note on the changed-file-scope bundle. This is review-tooling metadata only and has no effect on descriptor coverage, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, authority boundaries, or any pack deliverable; all substantive bindings and validations are correct.","recommendation":"Have the changed-file-scope generator exclude active-pack paths (docs/closeout-packs/<pack_id>/ and the active handoff doc) from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so downstream consumers are not ambiguous about pack membership.
